forecasts.reasoning: The Samoan government declared a 30-day state of emergency (SOE) due to power cuts on March 31, 2025, scheduled to end on April 29. For the SOE to be active on April 30, it must be officially extended. Resolving energy crises in Pacific island nations typically takes over 30 days due to the logistics of shipping replacement parts, fuel, or temporary generators from hubs like New Zealand or Australia. Furthermore, Samoa has a historical precedent of renewing SOEs when crises are ongoing (such as during the measles outbreak and COVID-19). However, because an SOE is often used primarily to fast-track emergency funds and procurement, it is possible the government will let it lapse if the necessary temporary contracts are already signed, even if power stability is not fully restored. Without clear confirmation of an extension by the knowledge cutoff (April 21, 2025), I estimate a 55% probability that the emergency declaration will be renewed or still in effect on April 30, balancing the logistical likelihood of persistent power issues with the possibility that the emergency administrative powers themselves are no longer required.

Without access to search tools, I must estimate based on the known state of the race as of April 2025. With several top contenders (Whitmer, Buttigieg, McDonald Rivet) bowing out of the 2026 Michigan Senate race as of mid-April, the field of potential Democratic candidates is narrowing. Haley Stevens is certainly a strong contender, alongside others like Mallory McMorrow and Hillary Scholten (or potentially Jocelyn Benson / Garlin Gilchrist, depending on the Governor race). However, the deadline of May 1, 2025, leaves only a 10-day window from the knowledge cutoff dates. Setting up a statewide campaign structure and choosing the optimal time to announce often takes weeks or months. While the pressure to fill the vacuum is high, the tight timeline makes it likelier than not that any announcement would happen after May 1, if she decides to run at all. Estimating her chances of running at roughly 30-40% and her chances of announcing specifically before May 1 at around 30-40% of that leads to a probability of roughly 14%.
